festivals.title: New Year 2026
festivals.description: The beginning of a new year. A time for reflection on the path of Dhamma and setting intentions for wisdom, compassion, and social justice.

festivals.title: Swami Vivekananda Jayanti
festivals.description: Birth anniversary of Swami Vivekananda, celebrated as National Youth Day. His 1893 Chicago speech introduced India's spiritual heritage to the world and inspired a generation.

festivals.title: Makar Sankranti
festivals.description: Festival marking the sun's transition into Makara rashi. Celebrated across India with diverse traditions symbolizing harvest, gratitude, and the triumph of light.

festivals.title: Indian Army Day
festivals.description: Marks the day Lt. Gen. K.M. Cariappa became the first Indian Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Army in 1949. Honors the bravery and sacrifice of India's armed forces.

festivals.title: National Girl Child Day
festivals.description: A day to raise awareness about the rights of the girl child, promote education for girls, and fight discrimination. Inspired by the values of equality championed by Dr. Ambedkar.

festivals.title: National Voters' Day
festivals.description: Established in 2011 to encourage young voters to participate in democracy. Dr. Ambedkar said: 'Political democracy cannot last unless there lies at its base social democracy.'

festivals.title: Republic Day
festivals.description: India adopted its Constitution on this day in 1950, drafted under the chairmanship of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar. A celebration of democracy, equality, and the sovereign republic.

festivals.title: Magha Puja
festivals.description: An important Buddhist festival commemorating the spontaneous gathering of 1,250 enlightened arahants before the Buddha at Veluvana, where he delivered the Ovadapatimokkha — the core principles of Buddhism.

festivals.title: Sant Ravidas Jayanti
festivals.description: Birth anniversary of Sant Ravidas (c. 1377–1527), the great Bhakti saint, poet, and social reformer who rejected caste hierarchy and preached the equality of all human beings.

festivals.title: Maitri Diwas
festivals.description: A day to celebrate universal love, compassion, and Maitri (loving-kindness) towards all beings — the core teaching of Buddha Dhamma.
